questions.question: What is the difference between a random variable and a discrete random variable?
questions.hint: A discrete random variable is a variable that can only take on specific values, whereas a random variable can take on any value in its sample space.

questions.question: A fair coin is flipped repeatedly until a head appears. What is the probability that the number of flips required to get the first head is greater than 2?
questions.hint: Use the geometric distribution to solve this problem.

questions.question: A bag contains 5 red balls and 3 blue balls. If one ball is drawn at random and without replacement, what is the probability that it is blue?
questions.hint: Use the concept of conditional probability to solve this problem.
